claimText: The Dhole (Asiatic Wild Dog) is distributed across multiple regions in India, including the Eastern and Western Ghats, Assam, Meghalaya, West Bengal, and Ladakh.
evidenceExcerpt: Habitat includes Eastern & Western Ghats, Assam, Meghalaya, West Bengal, Ladakh
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical distribution of Dholes in India.
distractorHooks: Not found in Ladakh or high altitude regions

claimText: The Dhole is classified as Endangered on the IUCN Red List, listed in CITES Appendix II, and protected under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act.
evidenceExcerpt: Conservation: WPA Schedule I, CITES Appendix II, IUCN Endangered.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on species conservation status.

claimText: The Dhole is a carnivorous animal that primarily hunts during the early morning or late evening.
evidenceExcerpt: Carnivorous, hunts in early morning or late evening.
examUse: Statement-based question on species behavior and hunting patterns.
